description_long: This is a vintage-style projector with a robust, boxy body finished in a matte brown color. The main housing is rectangular with rounded edges and sits atop a matching base, suggesting stability and portability. A prominent cylindrical lens assembly protrudes from the front, featuring a black ring around the lens opening. There are several small metallic screws and a visible seam along the side, indicating access panels or assembly points. A black rectangular logo plaque is affixed to one side, and subtle surface scuffs and wear marks are visible, hinting at frequent use. The top of the projector has a recessed area, possibly for ventilation or controls, and there are small vent-like slits on the upper edges.
description: A vintage, boxy brown projector with a protruding lens and a side logo plaque, featuring rounded edges and subtle wear marks.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the lens assembly, side logo plaque, and some vent slits on the top edge.
description_view_1: Left-side view highlighting the cylindrical lens, side panel seams, and minor surface scratches.
description_view_2: Rear-left view showing the plain back panel and the recessed top area, with minimal visible features.
description_view_3: Right-front view displaying the logo plaque, lens, and more of the top and side surface details.
attribute_brand_logo: A black rectangular plaque with a stylized emblem and possible text, located on the right side of the projector body. The brand name is not fully legible.
attribute_lens_type: fixed lens; cylindrical shape with a black ring, no visible adjustment rings or zoom features.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Narrow horizontal slits on the upper edges of the main body, likely for passive airflow.
